[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [41°3′6″N 73°31′14″W﻿ / ﻿41.05167°N 73.52056°W﻿ / 41.05167; -73.52056](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=St._Benedict%27s_Church_(Stamford,_Connecticut)&params=41_3_6_N_73_31_14_W_type:landmark_region:US-CT)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Historic church in Connecticut, United States

United States historic place

| Location | 1A St. Benedict's Circle, [Stamford, Connecticut](/wiki/Stamford,_Connecticut "Stamford, Connecticut") |
| Coordinates | [41°3′6″N 73°31′14″W﻿ / ﻿41.05167°N 73.52056°W﻿ / 41.05167; -73.52056](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=St._Benedict%27s_Church_(Stamford,_Connecticut)&params=41_3_6_N_73_31_14_W_type:landmark_region:US-CT) |
| Area | less than one acre |
| Built | 1930 |
| Architectural style | Neo-Tudor |
| [MPS](/wiki/Multiple_Property_Submission "Multiple Property Submission") | [Downtown Stamford Ecclesiastical Complexes TR](https://npgallery.nps.gov/NRHP/GetAsset/NRHP/64000089_text) |
| NRHP reference No. | [87002130](https://npgallery.nps.gov/AssetDetail/NRIS/87002130)[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1) |
| Added to NRHP | December 24, 1987 |

**Saint Benedict - Our Lady of Montserrat**, or simply **St. Benedict's Church**, is a [Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Church "Roman Catholic Church") church in [Stamford, Connecticut](/wiki/Stamford,_Connecticut "Stamford, Connecticut"), in the [Diocese of Bridgeport](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Bridgeport "Roman Catholic Diocese of Bridgeport"). The historic brick [Neo-Tudor](/wiki/Tudor_Revival_architecture "Tudor Revival architecture") [church](/wiki/Church_(building) "Church (building)") at 1A St. Benedict's Circle was built in 1930 and added to the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1987. The architect was [Henry F. Ludorf](/wiki/Henry_F._Ludorf "Henry F. Ludorf") of [Hartford, Connecticut](/wiki/Hartford,_Connecticut "Hartford, Connecticut"). The exterior uses a variety of building materials, including brick, ashlar stone, timbering, and stucco.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The church's main facade is asymmetrically arranged with its entrance on the left, under a handsome timber-frame porch, and a stone tower to the right which is topped by a bell-cot roof.[[3]](#cite_note-nrhpinv3-3)
